title: Gymnogramma filipendulifolia
intro: Gymnogramma filipendulifolia, commonly known as Gymnogramma, is a type of fern species, specifically a genus of flowering plants in the dandelion family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting the body from oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Gymnogramma is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Gymnogramma filipendulifolia is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. It is a type of flowering fern that belongs to the class Polypodiopsida. The genus Gymnogramma is part of the family Pteridaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as true ferns. Gymnogramma filipendulifolia is further classified into the order Polypodiales. This classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other ferns.
